backlog.title: Subcommands → flags + skills-first completions
backlog.description: Convert the three bare-word reserved subcommands (check, init, completion) into long-only --flags (--check, --init, --completions) so the bare positional namespace belongs entirely to skill tags (decision 19), then rewrite the three shell completion files to be skills-first and long-form-only (decision 20). The existing internal/ subsystems (discover, resolve, check, config, search, ui, skillsdir) are untouched except for one error-message string.

milestones.title: CLI contract conversion (check/init/completion → --check/--init/--completions)
milestones.description: The behavioral core. After M1, bare words are tags and the three actions are flags. All existing subsystems keep working; only the parsing/dispatch/error-message layer and tests change. M1.T1 is code; M1.T2 is usage/error strings; M1.T3 is tests.

tasks.title: parseArgs flag conversion + exclusivityError rewrite
tasks.description: Convert the parseArgs bare-subcommand cases to --flag cases, update config struct doc comments, and rewrite all exclusivityError messages. After T1, bare words fall through to tag capture and --check/--init/--completions select their modes. The dispatch logic in run() is unchanged — only how config fields get SET changes.

subtasks.title: parseArgs: delete bare cases, add --check/--init/--completions flag cases
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/004_5851dcff4371/architecture/code_change_map.md Change Group 1 for exact line numbers. The parseArgs function is at main.go:179-369. Three bare-word cases exist at lines 284 (check), 294 (completion), 324 (init). The `=`-form switch is at lines 196-234. The default tag-capture branch is at lines 353-368.
2. INPUT: The existing main.go file with working bare-word subcommand parsing. All tests currently pass (green baseline). The `=`-form switch already handles --store= and --shell= (which imply init/completion respectively).
3. LOGIC:
   a) DELETE the `case "check":` block (main.go:284-293), `case "completion":` block (main.go:294-299), and the ENTIRE `case "init":` block (main.go:324-351) including all Issue-4 duplicate-init logic and next != "check"/next != "completion" special-casing.
   b) ADD to the main switch a { } block three new cases:
      - `case "--check":` → `c.check = true`
      - `case "--completions":` → `c.completion = true` (NOTE: PLURAL, per decision 19 — the old subcommand was singular `completion`, the new flag is `--completions`)
      - `case "--init":` → `c.init = true`, then if i+1 < len(args) and args[i+1] does NOT start with "-", capture args[i+1] as c.initStore and i++ (mirrors the old `init <dir>` positional capture, now `--init <dir>`). A dashed following token (--init --store ...) is left for its own case.
   c) ADD to the `=`-form switch (lines 196-234) after the `--store=` case: `case "--init":` → `c.init = true; c.initStore = val; if val == "" { c.storeMissingValue = true }` (mirrors --store=).
   d) The default: branch (lines 353-368) NEEDS NO CHANGE — bare words check/init/completions now automatically land in c.tags.
   e) --store still implies init; --shell still implies completion — DO NOT change this wiring (lines 218-233, 300-323).
   f) Update config struct doc comments (main.go:162-167): replace "subcommand" with "flag" in the check/init/completion/initStore/completionShell field comments. Replace bare `skilldozer check` with `skilldozer --check`, `skilldozer init` with `skilldozer --init`, `skilldozer completion` with `skilldozer --completions`.
   g) Update package doc comment (main.go:10) — replace "subcommands like `check`" with "flags like `--check`".
   h) Remove/rewrite ALL code comments containing "reserved" or "subcommand" (~16 sites in non-test .go files — see code_change_map.md Change Group 6d). Replace with "flag" language.
4. OUTPUT: A main.go where parseArgs accepts --check/--init/--completions as flags and bare check/init/completions fall through to c.tags. go build ./... must succeed. go vet ./... must pass. Tests will FAIL until P1.M1.T3 — that is expected (TDD).
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update main.go doc comments on config struct fields and parseArgs cases to reference --check/--init/--completions flags instead of "subcommand". These ARE the developer-facing documentation for the CLI contract.

subtasks.title: exclusivityError: rewrite messages to --flags + mode-set consistency
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/004_5851dcff4371/architecture/code_change_map.md Change Group 3. The exclusivityError function is at main.go:782-835. The bool-driven gating LOGIC is unchanged (c.check/c.init/c.completion still gate the same families); only message strings and one mode-set literal change.
2. INPUT: main.go from P1.M1.T1.S1 (parseArgs already accepts flags). The exclusivityError function currently has messages referencing bare words: 'check' (lines 804, 807), 'init' (lines 815, 818), 'completion' (lines 825, 829).
3. LOGIC:
   a) Rewrite ALL six error messages: 'check' → '--check', 'init' → '--init', 'completion' → '--completions'. Example: line 804 "'check' cannot be combined with tag arguments" → "'--check' cannot be combined with tag arguments".
   b) Mode-set consistency: ADD c.completion to the init-block mode set at line 818 (currently `{c.check, c.list, c.searchMode, c.all, c.path}` — missing c.completion). The completion-block at line 829 backstops it today, but the delta requests consistency. Also add c.completion to the check-block mode set at line 807 if absent.
   c) Update the init-block message (line 818) to list --completions in the exclusion set: "'--init' cannot be combined with --check/--completions/--list/--search/--all/--path".
   d) Update the exclusivityError function doc comment (if it mentions "modes/subcommands") to use "flags" language.
4. OUTPUT: exclusivityError produces messages with --check/--init/--completions and all mode pairs are consistently caught. go build ./... must succeed.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] The error messages ARE the user-facing documentation for the exit-code contract (§6.4). No separate doc update needed.

tasks.title: usageText rewrite + error string updates + function doc comments
tasks.description: Rewrite the usageText constant (the --help / no-args surface) to show --check/--init/--completions flags, update all error prefix strings that reference bare 'init', and update function doc comments that reference the bare 'completion' subcommand.

subtasks.title: Rewrite usageText + update all error prefix strings + doc comments
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/004_5851dcff4371/architecture/code_change_map.md Change Groups 4-6 for exact line numbers.
2. INPUT: main.go from P1.M1.T1.S1 (parseArgs and exclusivityError already converted to flags).
3. LOGIC:
   a) usageText constant (main.go:71-117): Rewrite the USAGE block (lines 80-82): `skilldozer check` → `skilldozer --check`, `skilldozer init [<dir>]` → `skilldozer --init [<dir>]`, `skilldozer completion [--shell <name>]` → `skilldozer --completions [--shell <name>]`. Rewrite EXAMPLES (lines 95-97): `skilldozer check` → `skilldozer --check`, `skilldozer init --store <dir>` → `skilldozer --init --store <dir>`, `eval "$(skilldozer completion)"` → `eval "$(skilldozer --completions)"`. Rewrite OPTIONS (lines 104-108): bare `check` → `--check`, bare `init [<dir>]` → `--init [<dir>]`, bare `completion [--shell <name>]` → `--completions [--shell <name>]`. Add a note after USAGE or before OPTIONS: "Help and completions advertise long forms only; short aliases (-a, -l, -s, -f, -p, -h, -v) remain valid for typing but are not advertised (§6.1)."
   b) skillsdir.ErrNotFound (internal/skillsdir/skillsdir.go:275): Change message from `"skilldozer is not configured; run \`skilldozer init\`"` to `"skilldozer is not configured; run \`skilldozer --init\`"`. This is the ONLY change to any internal/ file.
   c) runInit/setupStore error prefixes (8 sites in main.go): ALL occurrences of `"skilldozer init: "` → `"skilldozer --init: "`. Exact lines: 1001, 1005, 1027, 1090, 1095, 1100, 1103, 1110.
   d) Comment-only references (3 sites): lines 1086, 1150, 1162 — update `"skilldozer init: ..."` references to `"skilldozer --init: ..."`.
   e) Function doc comments: completionScript (main.go:1112-1120), detectShell (main.go:1234-1243), runCompletion (main.go:1257-1271) — update all references from `skilldozer completion` to `skilldozer --completions` and from `eval "$(skilldozer completion)"` to `eval "$(skilldozer --completions)"`. NOTE: function NAMES stay as-is (completionScript, detectShell, runCompletion) — only doc comments change.
   f) runCheck doc comment (main.go:631): update from `skilldozer check` to `skilldozer --check`.
4. OUTPUT: A main.go where usageText shows only --flag forms, all error strings reference --init/--check/--completions, and all doc comments use flag language. go build ./... must succeed.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] The usageText constant IS the user-facing --help surface. The error prefix strings ARE the user-facing error messages. These updates ride WITH the work — no separate docs subtask.

tasks.title: Update main_test.go to the --flag contract
tasks.description: Flip every test that passes a bare check/init/completion token expecting a mode to instead pass --check/--init/--completions. Update all error-message assertions to reference --flags. Add new tests for namespace safety (bare words now land in c.tags). After T3, go test ./... is fully green.

subtasks.title: Flip parseArgs-level + exclusivity-level tests to --flag contract
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/004_5851dcff4371/architecture/test_doc_change_map.md for the complete list of test functions with exact line numbers. ~15 parseArgs-level and ~19 exclusivity-level functions need flipping. The test helper pattern uses standalone one-function-per-case style (not table-driven) for parseArgs capture tests.
2. INPUT: The main.go from P1.M1.T1 + P1.M1.T2 where parseArgs accepts --check/--init/--completions and bare words fall through to c.tags. exclusivityError produces --flag messages.
3. LOGIC:
   a) Flip ~15 parseArgs-level test functions (lines 1224-1457): every test passing bare `"check"`/`"init"`/`"completion"` tokens must pass `"--check"`/`"--init"`/`"--completions"` instead. See test_doc_change_map.md for the exact mapping. Examples: TestParseArgsCheckSubcommand (1224) → pass `"--check"`, assert c.check==true. TestParseArgsInitSubcommand (1262) → pass `"--init"`. TestParseArgsCompletionSubcommand (1389) → pass `"--completions"`. TestParseArgsInitPositionalDir (1279) → pass `"--init", "/tmp/x"`.
   b) RENAME test functions from "Subcommand" to "Flag" (e.g., TestParseArgsCheckSubcommand → TestParseArgsCheckFlag, TestParseArgsInitSubcommand → TestParseArgsInitFlag, TestParseArgsCompletionSubcommand → TestParseArgsCompletionsFlag).
   c) Flip ~19 exclusivity test functions (lines 1795-2115): every test passing bare tokens must pass flags. Update ALL error message assertions: 'check' → '--check', 'init' → '--init', 'completion' → '--completions'. See test_doc_change_map.md for the exact mapping per function.
   d) Handle TestRunExclusivityInitInit (2006): old test passes `"init", "init"` expecting exit 2 (init+tags conflict via Issue-4 logic). NEW: pass `"--init", "--init"` → the second `--init` is a duplicate flag, not a tag. The behavior depends on how parseArgs handles duplicate --init: likely c.init is set twice (idempotent), no tags captured, no exclusivity error → falls through to init dispatch. ALTERNATIVELY, the old init+tags conflict is now `--init sometag` (a bare tag after --init flag). Rewrite this test to `"--init", "sometag"` → c.init=true, c.tags=["sometag"] → exclusivity catches init+tags → exit 2.
   e) Add NEW namespace-safety tests: TestParseArgsBareCheckNowTag — bare `"check"` → c.check==false, c.tags==["check"]. TestParseArgsBareInitNowTag — bare `"init"` → c.init==false, c.tags==["init"]. TestParseArgsBareCompletionsNowTag — bare `"completions"` → c.completion==false, c.tags==["completions"]. TestParseArgsInitFlagWithDir — `"--init", "/tmp/x"` → c.init==true, c.initStore=="/tmp/x", len(c.tags)==0. TestParseArgsInitEqualsDir — `"--init=/tmp/x"` → c.init==true, c.initStore=="/tmp/x".
4. OUTPUT: go test ./... passes with all parseArgs-level and exclusivity-level tests flipped to the --flag contract. New namespace-safety tests are green.
5. DOCS: none — no user-facing/config/API surface change (test-only).

subtasks.title: Flip run-level dispatch + completion tests + help-text assertions
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/004_5851dcff4371/architecture/test_doc_change_map.md for the complete list. ~11 run-level dispatch functions + ~6 completion functions + 2 help-text functions + 1 unconfigured test need flipping.
2. INPUT: The main.go from P1.M1.T1-T2 with --flag parsing. The main_test.go from P1.M1.T3.S1 with parseArgs/exclusivity tests already flipped.
3. LOGIC:
   a) Flip ~11 run-level dispatch test functions (TestRunCheck* lines 1474-1637, TestRunInit* lines 2760-2820): every test calling run() with bare `"check"`/`"init"` tokens must pass `"--check"`/`"--init"` instead. The assertions (exit codes, stdout/stderr content) remain the same — only the input arg changes.
   b) Flip ~6 completion dispatch tests (TestRunCompletion* lines 2953-3037): every test passing `"completion"` must pass `"--completions"`. Examples: TestRunCompletionBashScript (2953) → run([]string{"--completions", "--shell", "bash"}, ...). TestRunCompletionUnsupportedShell (2986) → run([]string{"--completions", "--shell", "tcsh"}, ...).
   c) Update help-text tests: TestRunHelpShowsInitRow (2029) — assert stdout contains `--init` (not bare `init`). TestRunHelpShowsCompletionRow (2116) — assert stdout contains `--completions` (not bare `completion`).
   d) Update TestRunBareTagUnconfiguredNeverPrompts (2867): assert stderr contains `run \`skilldozer --init\`` (not bare `init`).
   e) Verify TestEmbeddedCompletionsMatchOnDisk (2929) still passes — it reads on-disk completion files and compares to embedded vars. It will still pass (the files haven't changed yet — that's M2.T1).
   f) Run the FULL test suite: go test ./... must be 100% green. No existing green test may regress.
4. OUTPUT: go test ./... is fully green. All bare-subcommand references in tests are eliminated. Namespace-safety is verified.
5. DOCS: none — no user-facing/config/API surface change (test-only).

milestones.title: User-facing surfaces: completions rewrite + README sync
milestones.description: Rewrite the three shell completion files to be skills-first and long-form-only (decision 20), then sync README.md to reflect the new --flag CLI contract. M2 depends on M1 via the §14.4 lockstep invariant (completion files are frozen to parseArgs).

tasks.title: Rewrite the three completion files (skills-first + long-form-only)
tasks.description: Rewrite completions/skilldozer.bash, completions/_skilldozer (zsh), and completions/skilldozer.fish to: (a) remove bare subcommand offers so a bare <tab> yields skills only, (b) remove short-form flag offers (-v -h -p -l -a -f -s) so flags are long-form-only, (c) add --check/--init/--completions to each file's flag set, (d) keep tag completion (the skilldozer --relative --all 2>/dev/null probe) byte-identical. A rebuild is required for --completions to emit the new bytes (§14.6 lockstep).

subtasks.title: Rewrite completions/skilldozer.bash (skills-first, long-form-only)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/004_5851dcff4371/architecture/completions_change_map.md §1 for exact line numbers. The bash completion is 69 lines. Key areas: flag list in compgen -W at line 41, value-routing at lines 33-36, walk-guard at lines 46-55, tag probe at line 61, subcommand offers at line 63.
2. INPUT: The main.go from P1.M1.T1 where parseArgs accepts --check/--init/--completions as flags and bare words are tags. The §14.4 lockstep invariant requires the bash flag set to match parseArgs exactly.
3. LOGIC:
   a) Flag list (line 41, compgen -W): Remove ALL 7 short forms (-v -h -p -l -a -f -s). Keep long forms (--version --help --path --list --all --file --relative --no-color --search --store). ADD three new flags: --check --init --completions. Final word list: "--version --help --path --list --all --file --relative --no-color --search --store --check --init --completions".
   b) Value-routing (line 34): Remove -s from --search routing: `--search|-s)` → `--search)`. Add --init to directory routing: `--store)` → `--store|--init) COMPREPLY=($(compgen -d -- "$cur")); return 0`.
   c) Walk-guard (lines 46-55): Remove the `check`/`init`/`completion` bare-word suppression from line 52. Simplify the have_pos tracking — without subcommand exclusivity, positionals are always skills and skills are never mutually exclusive with each other (you can pass multiple tags to skilldozer <tag> [<tag>...]).
   d) Subcommand offers (line 63): DELETE `cands="$cands check init completion"`. Line becomes just `cands="$tags"` (or the line is removed and the variable flows directly).
   e) Tag probe (line 61): KEEP BYTE-IDENTICAL: `tags=$(skilldozer --relative --all 2>/dev/null)`.
   f) LOCKSTEP comment (lines 11-13): Update to cite decisions 19/20 and the long-form-only rule: "Flags are long-form-only (decision 20); short aliases stay valid at runtime but are not advertised. Updated for --check/--init/--completions (decision 19)."
4. OUTPUT: A bash completion file where bare <tab> shows skills only, skilldozer -<tab> shows long-form flags only (including --check/--init/--completions), and tag completion is byte-identical. The §13 delta assertions must pass after go build.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] This file IS the user-facing tab-completion surface. The LOCKSTEP comment IS the developer-facing maintenance doc.

subtasks.title: Rewrite completions/_skilldozer (zsh) + completions/skilldozer.fish
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/004_5851dcff4371/architecture/completions_change_map.md §2 (zsh, 61 lines) and §3 (fish, 52 lines) for exact line numbers.
2. INPUT: The main.go from P1.M1.T1 (flag contract). The bash completion from P1.M2.T1.S1 (use as the reference pattern for the flag list and LOCKSTEP comment).
3. LOGIC — ZSH (completions/_skilldozer):
   a) Flag array (lines 21-39): Remove ALL short-form entries. Lines 22-27 currently have pairs like `'--version[...]' '-v[...]'` — delete the `-v`/`-h`/`-p`/`-l`/`-a`/`-f` right-hand entries, keeping only the `--long` left-hand entries. Delete line 34 (the `-s[...]` standalone entry for search), keeping line 33 (`'--search[...]:query:'`).
   b) Add three new long flags to the array (before the closing `)` at ~line 38): `'--check[Validate every skill on disk]'`, `'--init[First-run setup: pick/create the skills store]:directory:_files'`, `'--completions[Emit the shell completion script for eval]'`.
   c) State handling (lines 43-58): REMOVE the `check init completion` from `compadd` at line 46 → `compadd -- "$tags[@]"`. Simplify the first/rest split — without subcommand exclusivity, all positionals are skills: replace the case block with a simple `'*: :->args'` state that always `compadd -- "$tags[@]"`. Remove the exclusivity guard at line 52.
   d) Tag probe (line 19): KEEP BYTE-IDENTICAL.
   e) LOCKSTEP comment (lines 12-14): Update to match bash's updated comment.
4. LOGIC — FISH (completions/skilldozer.fish):
   a) Flag matrix (lines 17-22): Remove ALL `-s` short opts. Lines 17-22 have `-s v -l version` etc. — remove the `-s X` tokens, keeping only `-l longform`. Line 32: remove `-s s` from search, keeping `-l search`.
   b) Add three new flags after line 24: `complete -c skilldozer -l check -d 'Validate every skill on disk'`, `complete -c skilldozer -l init -d 'First-run setup: pick/create the skills store' -r`, `complete -c skilldozer -l completions -d 'Emit the shell completion script for eval'`.
   c) Add --init to directory routing: after line 39 (`complete -c skilldozer -l store ... -r`), the --init flag should also trigger directory completion. Fish handles this automatically via `-r` on the --init flag definition.
   d) DELETE bare subcommand offers (lines 43-45): Remove all three `__fish_is_first_arg -a 'check'/'init'/'completion'` directives.
   e) Tag directive guard (lines 51-52): Re-author ONLY the `-n` condition — remove `__fish_seen_subcommand_from check init completion` (no subcommands to guard against) and remove `-s` from `--search -s` reference. The `-a '(skilldozer --relative --all 2>/dev/null)'` probe MUST stay byte-identical. New guard: `complete -c skilldozer -n 'not __fish_prev_arg_in --search' \`.
   f) LOCKSTEP comment: Update to match bash's updated comment.
5. OUTPUT: zsh and fish completion files where bare <tab> shows skills only and flags are long-form-only (including --check/--init/--completions). Tag probes byte-identical.
6. DOCS: [Mode A] These files ARE the user-facing tab-completion surfaces. LOCKSTEP comments ARE the developer-facing maintenance docs.

tasks.title: Sync changeset-level documentation
tasks.description: Mode B changeset-level documentation: update README.md to reflect the new --flag CLI contract. This task depends on ALL implementing subtasks (it summarizes the entire changeset). Update every command reference, remove the now-obsolete 'Reserved tag names' section, and document the skills-first completions behavior.

subtasks.title: Update README.md: command refs, remove Reserved section, completions section
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/004_5851dcff4371/architecture/test_doc_change_map.md §README for exact line numbers. README.md is 345 lines. The 'Reserved tag names' paragraph is at lines 239-247. ~16 lines reference bare subcommands.
2. INPUT: The completed codebase from M1 (all tests green, --flag contract active) and M2.T1 (completion files rewritten). This is a documentation-only subtask — no code changes.
3. LOGIC:
   a) Update ALL bare subcommand references (~16 lines): `skilldozer init` → `skilldozer --init`, `skilldozer check` → `skilldozer --check`, `skilldozer completion` → `skilldozer --completions`. Exact lines: 43, 63, 66, 76, 77, 89, 107, 113, 117, 183, 240, 281, 297, 315, 330. Also scan for any `eval "$(skilldozer completion)"` → `eval "$(skilldozer --completions)"` and `skilldozer completion --shell fish | source` → `skilldozer --completions --shell fish | source`.
   b) DELETE the 'Reserved tag names' paragraph (lines 239-247) entirely — it is now factually wrong. There are NO reserved names. Replace with a one-line note if useful: "Bare words are always skill tags; actions are --flags (§6.1). A skill named check/init/completions resolves normally."
   c) Update the Completions section (lines 94-150): The eval/source one-liners are now `eval "$(skilldozer --completions)"` (bash/zsh) and `skilldozer --completions --shell fish | source` (fish). Describe the skills-first + long-form-only behavior: a bare <tab> lists your skills; skilldozer -<tab> lists the long-form flags (--all, --check, --completions, --file, --help, --init, --list, --no-color, --path, --relative, --search, --store, --version), narrowed by what you type. Note that --init/--check/--completions are flags (decision 19) and short forms are valid but not advertised (decision 20).
   d) Verify README section ordering matches PRD §15 outline: Title+one-liner, Why, Install, Usage, Where skills live, Adding a skill, How skilldozer finds the store, Shell completions, Constraints.
   e) Verify with grep assertions: `grep -q 'skilldozer --completions' README.md` (new commands present), `! grep -q 'Reserved tag names' README.md` (old section removed), `! grep -Eq 'skilldozer (init|check|completion)\b' README.md` (no bare subcommands remain, word-boundary safe against --init/--check/--completions).
4. OUTPUT: A README.md that accurately documents the --flag CLI contract with no stale bare-subcommand references. All grep assertions pass.
5. DOCS: This IS the Mode B changeset-level documentation task. It depends on all implementing subtasks and runs last.

backlog.title: Symlink-aware discovery + derived zsh completions emit (PRD §7.1 / §14.6 mid-session integration)
backlog.description: Two PRD edits landed mid-session AFTER the P1 baseline (subcommands → flags). Both are ALREADY satisfied by shipped code and `go test ./...` is green, so this phase FORMALIZES them in the breakdown rather than scheduling re-work. P1 is preserved untouched — neither change touches the parsing/dispatch/completion-FILE layer P1 owned, so no 'UPDATE REQUIRED' note is added to any P1 task. (1) §7.1 now requires the discovery walk to FOLLOW symlinked directories: a skill reachable only through a symlink is discovered and reported under its on-disk link name (not the resolved target), and cycles are broken via a visited set of resolved (canonical) paths. Shipped in internal/discover/index.go (Index). (2) §14.6 now requires the `--completions` emit path to DERIVE an eval-safe zsh wrapper from the embedded autoload file (bash/fish stay verbatim); completionScript returns verbatim bytes locked by TestEmbeddedCompletionsMatchOnDisk. Shipped in main.go (zshEvalScript + runCompletion). NOTE: the code predates/parallels the formal PRD edit — the PRD caught up to the implementation, not the reverse; tasks are marked Complete to reflect verified reality and avoid an implementer redoing finished work ('without losing progress').

milestones.title: Symlink-aware discovery walk (§7.1)
milestones.description: discover.Index must follow symlinked directories so a skill reachable only via a symlink is discovered and reported under its on-disk link name (not the resolved EvalSymlinks target), and must terminate when a link points back at an ancestor (cycle). Shipped in internal/discover/index.go; locked by the index_test.go symlink suite. No change to the frontmatter parser (discover.go), Skill extraction (skill.go), or any consumer — only the walk; --all/--relative --all, --list, resolve, --search, and --check all inherit symlink support automatically through []Skill.

tasks.title: Implement symlink-following + cycle-guarded Index walk
tasks.description: Rewrite the recursive scan so linked directories are entered (filepath.WalkDir skips them by default), RelTag/Dir are reported through the link name, and a canonical-path visited set stops cycles. Keep the manifest-free contract (rebuilt from disk each call) and the per-entry error-skip policy.

subtasks.title: Index: symlink-following walk with dual-path tracking + canonical-path cycle guard
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: The walk lives in internal/discover/index.go Index(skillsDir). It threads TWO path coordinate systems: displayPath (the path AS IT APPEARS under skillsDir, symlinks preserved — backs RelTag and Skill.Dir) and realPath (the EvalSymlinks canonical target — what is read, and the cycle-detection key). The visited set is map[string]bool keyed by canonical real path, seeded with realRoot. Unlike filepath.WalkDir (which skips linked dirs by default), this walk resolves every entry with EvalSymlinks and recurses.
2. INPUT: §7.1 step 2 (updated PRD): 'Symlinked directories ARE followed — a skill reachable only through a symlink is discovered and reported under its on-disk link name (not the resolved target). Cycles are broken via a visited set of resolved (canonical) paths, so a link that points back at an ancestor terminates the walk instead of looping forever.'
3. LOGIC (as shipped):
   a) os.Stat (not Lstat) on root so a skills dir that is itself a symlink-to-dir is accepted.
   b) For each entry with ModeSymlink set, filepath.EvalSymlinks resolves childReal to the canonical target; broken/unresolvable links are skipped (Stat/EvalSymlinks error → continue), and isDir is taken from the resolved Stat.
   c) A directory directly containing SKILL.md (plain OR a link to a dir) is a skill; its RelTag is filepath.Rel(root, displayPath) with filepath.ToSlash — the on-disk LINK NAME, not the resolved target. This is the §7.1 'reported under its on-disk link name' requirement.
   d) Cycle guard: before recursing into a dir, check visited[childReal] (canonical real path); if present skip, else mark and recurse. EvalSymlinks canonicalizes, so a link back at an ancestor resolves to a string already in the set → terminates instead of looping forever.
   e) Per-entry errors (unreadable subtree) are skipped; malformed-YAML SKILL.md still yields a HasFM=false Skill (lenient; check re-parses SourceFile). Manifest-free contract unchanged (rebuilt from disk each call).
4. OUTPUT: discover.Index follows symlinks, reports skills under link names, and terminates on cycles. No consumer changes required — all modes inherit symlink support through []Skill.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] The Index doc comment in internal/discover/index.go documents the dual-path model and cycle guard — it is the developer-facing reference for §7.1.

subtasks.title: Tests: symlink discovery, nested-tree link, cycle termination, broken link, symlinked root
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: internal/discover/index_test.go has a symlink suite gated by mustSymlink (t.Skip when the platform/filesystem can't create links): TestIndexFollowsSymlinkedSkillDir, TestIndexSymlinkedNestedTree, TestIndexSymlinkCycleBroken (5s deadline), TestIndexBrokenSymlinkSkipped, TestIndexSymlinkedRoot.
2. INPUT: The shipped Index from P2.M1.T1.S1.
3. LOGIC (as shipped, all PASSING — verified by `go test ./internal/discover`):
   a) TestIndexFollowsSymlinkedSkillDir: a skill dir OUTSIDE the root, linked in by name, is discovered; Skill.Dir is the display path THROUGH the symlink (the link name, not the resolved target); SourceFile is readable through the link; frontmatter is parsed.
   b) TestIndexSymlinkedNestedTree: a symlink to a multi-skill tree is discovered with all nested skills, tags under the link name.
   c) TestIndexSymlinkCycleBroken: two cycles planted (link→root, link→sub-ancestor); the 5s-deadline Index call returns instead of looping forever.
   d) TestIndexBrokenSymlinkSkipped: a dangling symlink is skipped silently (nil error, not a skill).
   e) TestIndexSymlinkedRoot: a skills dir that is ITSELF a symlink-to-dir works; tags reported under the link root.
4. OUTPUT: go test ./internal/discover passes on a symlink-capable platform (and skips cleanly elsewhere). §7.1 symlink contract is locked.
5. DOCS: none (test-only).

milestones.title: Derived (eval-safe) zsh completions emit (§14.6)
milestones.description: The `--completions` emit path must return the embedded bytes VERBATIM for bash and fish, but DERIVE an eval-safe zsh wrapper from the embedded autoload file: strip the trailing `_skilldozer "$@"` self-call and append an explicit `compdef _skilldozer skilldozer` plus a guarded `compinit` bootstrap. completionScript stays verbatim (byte-identity lock via TestEmbeddedCompletionsMatchOnDisk); the derivation lives in runCompletion/zshEvalScript. The §14.5 manual fpath path keeps using the verbatim on-disk autoload file (it is correct as an autoload — the self-call is the standard idiom that fires once compsys loads). Shipped in main.go; locked by main_test.go.

tasks.title: zshEvalScript derivation + runCompletion branch + verbatim lock
tasks.description: Add the eval-safe derivation for zsh while keeping bash/fish verbatim and keeping completionScript's return value byte-identical to the on-disk autoload file. Depends on P1.M2.T1.S2 — the on-disk completions/_skilldozer autoload file this derives from; its trailing `_skilldozer "$@"` self-call is the strip target (already present in P1.M2.T1.S2's shipped file, so no re-work is needed there).

subtasks.title: zshEvalScript + zshEvalRegistration; completionScript verbatim; runCompletion derives zsh only
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: main.go has completionScript(shell) (returns the //go:embed vars bashCompletion/zshCompletion/fishCompletion verbatim, with a true/false by-shell bool), zshEvalScript(raw string) string, the const zshEvalRegistration, and runCompletion (the `skilldozer --completions` handler). runCompletion derives ONLY for zsh: `script, ok := completionScript(shell); ...; if shell == "zsh" { script = zshEvalScript(script) }`; bash and fish are emitted verbatim.
2. INPUT: §14.6 'Embedding (self-sufficient binary)' plus the NEW 'bash/fish vs zsh emit path' blockquote: under `eval "$(skilldozer --completions)"` the `#compdef skilldozer` header is an inert comment and the trailing `_skilldozer "$@"` self-call runs the function immediately, hitting `_arguments` before compsys is guaranteed loaded (`_skilldozer:31: command not found: _arguments`). The §14.5 manual fpath path (copy onto fpath + compinit) keeps using the verbatim autoload file; only the `--completions` eval path uses the derived wrapper.
3. LOGIC (as shipped):
   a) zshEvalScript strips the trailing `_skilldozer "$@"` self-call via strings.LastIndex (robust to trailing-comment drift; the function body never references its own name, so the single match is the self-call), then TrimRight collapses blank lines so the body ends cleanly at the closing brace.
   b) Appends zshEvalRegistration: `autoload -Uz compinit`, `(( $+functions[compdef] )) || compinit`, `(( $+functions[compdef] )) && compdef _skilldozer skilldozer` — a guarded bootstrap that is a no-op once compsys is already loaded (oh-my-zsh / prezto / a manual `autoload -U compinit && compinit` all define compdef).
   c) completionScript is UNCHANGED: it returns the embedded bytes verbatim for all three shells. The derivation lives in runCompletion only, preserving the §14.6 byte-identity lock (on-disk file == embedded var == completionScript output).
   d) Bash and fish emit paths are byte-identical to the §14.5 manual source/copy path.
4. OUTPUT: `eval "$(skilldozer --completions)"` in ~/.zshrc no longer triggers `_skilldozer:31: command not found: _arguments`; bash/fish behavior unchanged.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] The completionScript, zshEvalScript, and zshEvalRegistration doc comments in main.go document the verbatim-vs-derived split — they are the developer-facing reference for §14.6. Function NAMES (completionScript/detectShell/runCompletion) are unchanged (internal; renaming was out of scope in P1 and stays so).
6. NOTE: This formalizes already-shipped code; the on-disk autoload file from P1.M2.T1.S2 already ends with the self-call the derivation strips (verified by TestZshEvalScriptStripsSelfCall passing against the real embedded file).

subtasks.title: Tests: verbatim lock, self-call strip, compdef registration, runCompletion zsh eval-safety
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: main_test.go has TestEmbeddedCompletionsMatchOnDisk (reads on-disk completions/* and asserts byte-equality with the embedded vars — the §14.6 byte-identity lock), TestZshEvalScriptStripsSelfCall, TestZshEvalScriptRegistersCompdef, TestRunCompletionZshIsEvalSafe, plus the existing TestRunCompletionBashScript / FishScript / UnsupportedShell / UndetectableShell / EnvShellDetected / LoginShellDetected (§6.4 exit-code paths, unchanged by this work).
2. INPUT: The shipped zshEvalScript/runCompletion from P2.M2.T1.S1.
3. LOGIC (as shipped, all PASSING — verified by `go test ./...`):
   a) TestEmbeddedCompletionsMatchOnDisk: completionScript's source (the embedded vars) is byte-identical to the on-disk completions/* files — locks the 'single source of truth' invariant even with the zsh derivation in place (the derivation is applied AFTER completionScript returns verbatim).
   b) TestZshEvalScriptStripsSelfCall: the eval output does NOT contain the `_skilldozer "$@"` self-call (the _arguments bug source).
   c) TestZshEvalScriptRegistersCompdef: the eval output contains `compdef _skilldozer skilldozer` and keeps the `#compdef skilldozer` header and the `_arguments` call in the function body.
   d) TestRunCompletionZshIsEvalSafe: end-to-end — runCompletion for zsh emits a script containing `compdef _skilldozer skilldozer`.
4. OUTPUT: go test ./... is green; the §14.6 derived-zsh contract is locked without regressing bash/fish or the §6.4 exit-code matrix.
5. DOCS: none (test-only).
